raw.objective: to evaluate: 1) “the effect of therapeutic hypothermia on phenobarbital pharmacokinetics in asphyxiated neonates” 2) “the clinical efficacy of phenobarbital under hypothermia” 3) “whether phenobarbital has a potential depressing effect on the aEEG background pattern in this hypothermic population”
raw.covariates: Included: WT on CL and Vd. Drug effect on transition from continuous normal voltage to discontinuous normal voltage aEEG pattern
raw.observations: Drug effect was only found to affect transition between aEEG states in an on/off style, meaning that no dose or concentration-dependent effect could be observed, even though it is remarked that concentrations below 20 mg/L resulted in significantly more seizures.
raw.dose: Single and multiple IV in patients
raw.structure: [PK] 1 compartment with first order elimination [PD] 3 compartment first order transitions
raw.equations: [PK] CL=theta1*((WT/3.5)**theta3)*exp(eta1) Vd=theta2*((WT/3.5)**theta4)*exp(eta2) Cobs=Cipred*(1+eps1)+eps2 [PD] Edir=theta9 K34=theta5 K43=theta6 K45=theta7 K54=theta8*(1+Edir) DADT1= K43*A2 -K34*A1 DADT2= K34*A1 +K54*A3 -K43*A2 -K45*A2 DADT3= K45*A2 -K54*A3 if(dv<4) Yipred=A1 if(dv==4) Yipred=A2 if(dv==5) Yipred=A3 Yobs=Yipred*(1+eps1) Edir: only the theta9 was reported without a mathematical description of its relationship to PHB concentration. P5 and P4 seem to even out in their simulations at a dose of 40 mg/kg, with half of the difference roughly occurring at 20 mg/kg. From this we could conclude that in fact the following should be used instead of what is written above: Edir=((-0.041*C)/(C+20)) K54=theta8+Edir
raw.parameter_values: theta1=0.0172 theta2=3.45 theta3=0.81 theta4=1.08 theta5=0.035 theta6=0.007 theta7=0.051 theta8=0.091 theta9=-0.041 omega1=0.17 omega2=0.0065 sigma1=0.0024

structural_model.description: [PK] 1 compartment with first order elimination [PD] 3 compartment first order transitions

omega.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending
Appendix NONMEM-scale OMEGA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

sigma.conversion: Appendix NONMEM-scale SIGMA value retained verbatim; source-paper verification pending

P5 and P4 seem to even out in their simulations at a dose of 40 mg/kg, with half of the difference roughly occurring at 20 mg/kg. From this we could conclude that in fact the following should be used instead of what is written above: Edir=((-0.041*C)/(C+20)) K54=theta8+Edir
extraction_target.notes: Appendix-derived silver reference; compare with the publication before promotion to a gold tier.

study.dose: Single and multiple IV in patients
study.objective: to evaluate: 1) “the effect of therapeutic hypothermia on phenobarbital pharmacokinetics in asphyxiated neonates” 2) “the clinical efficacy of phenobarbital under hypothermia” 3) “whether phenobarbital has a potential depressing effect on the aEEG background pattern in this hypothermic population”

covariates.included: WT on CL and Vd. Drug effect on transition from continuous normal voltage to discontinuous normal voltage aEEG pattern
covariates.raw: Included: WT on CL and Vd. Drug effect on transition from continuous normal voltage to discontinuous normal voltage aEEG pattern

normalization.omega: Values labelled OMEGA in the appendix are retained as NONMEM variances; no CV reconversion is applied.
normalization.eta: ETA distribution is inferred only when the appendix equation explicitly uses exp(ETA) or additive ETA.
normalization.source_text: The appendix row is preserved verbatim in raw; normalized fields never replace it.
